title: cyphostemma rutilans
intro: Cyphostemma rutilans, also known as the "River Bushman's Poison" or "Wild Waterberry", is a plant species native to southern Africa, characterized by its red stems and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Cyphostemma rutilans include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Cyphostemma rutilans is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also consumed raw or used as a topical application.
